input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyApp.Views"
    Title="MyApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard: single page in the flyout -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products: two tabs (Active / Archived) -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to show them how to set up AppShell.xaml for a .NET MAUI app with:
1. A flyout menu with two sections:
   - "Dashboard" (single page)
   - "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and "Archived")
2. A Settings page accessible from the flyout
3. Route registration for a ProductDetailsPage

Let me create a clear example.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyApp.Views"
    Title="MyApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard: single page in the flyout -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products: two tabs (Active / Archived) -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the setup:

**AppShell.xaml** — defines the flyout structure:
- **Dashboard** → single `ShellContent` page
- **Products** → a `Tab` with two `ShellContent` items ("Active" / "Archived"), which renders as bottom tabs within that flyout item
- **Settings** → single page in the flyout

**AppShell.xaml.cs** — registers the detail route:

```csharp
Routing.RegisterRoute("productdetails", typeof(Views.ProductDetailsPage));
```

**Navigating to details** from either products page:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"productdetails?id={product.Id}");
```

Receive the parameter in `ProductDetailsPage` with:

```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(ProductId), "id")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    public string ProductId { get; set; }
}
```

Key points:
- `FlyoutItem` → top-level flyout entry
- `Tab` with multiple `ShellContent` → bottom tabs within a flyout item
- `Routing.RegisterRoute` → for pages pushed onto the navigation stack (not in the shell hierarchy)
